questions.question: Who among the following decides the allocation of portfolios among Indian state ministers?
questions.option_2: Chief Justice of the High Court

questions.question: What is a key obligation of citizens toward fellow citizens and society?
questions.option_1: Serve in the military automatically and responsibly
questions.option_3: Participate morally and socially, not just legally

questions.question: In which year was the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) formed under the Disaster Management Act of 2005?
Which example best reflects the need for the Constitution in daily life?
questions.option_1: Buying groceries from a local store
questions.option_2: Class deciding rules for representation and resource sharing

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment added the words 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
Which of the following cases CANNOT be taken up by a Lok Adalat?
In which of the following years was the National Telecom Policy (NTP), that envisaged the objective of one public call office (PCO) for every 500 persons in the urban areas, formulated?
Which of the following Articles deals with the Right against Exploitation?
Which Article was inserted into the Constitution by the 103ʳᵈ Amendment Act to provide for EWS reservation?
In which year was the Second Pay Commission appointed in India?
Which of the following statements is correct about Article 323B of the Constitution?
questions.option_1: It allows the creation of tribunals only for the armed forces.
questions.option_2: It permits tribunals for matters like taxation, land reforms, and labour disputes.
questions.option_3: It restricts tribunals to environmental cases only.
questions.option_4: It empowers states to establish tribunals for local governance.

questions.question: Which of the following subjects falls under the Concurrent List?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions the Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President?
Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is provided under Article 40 of the Indian Constitution?
questions.option_2: Provision for a uniform civil code
questions.option_3: Promotion of cottage industries in rural areas
questions.option_4: Organisation of agriculture and animal husbandry

questions.question: Which schedule of the Citizenship Act, 1955, contains the qualifications for naturalisation?
Which of the following options correctly lists the composition of the State Executive in India?
questions.option_1: Governor, Chief Minister and Council of Ministers
questions.option_3: Governor, Chief Minister and State Legislative Assembly members
questions.option_4: Governor, Deputy Minister and State Council of Advisors

questions.question: If an RTI applicant is aggrieved by the Public Information Officer’s decision (or lack of response), within how many days must the first appeal be filed under Section 19 of the RTI Act?
Select the correct statement regarding the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) of India.
questions.option_1: It is a constitutional body formed by a Constitutional Amendment, with powers equal to the Supreme Court.
questions.option_2: The NHRC has direct powers to prosecute and punish public officials for human rights abuses.
questions.option_3: It was established in 1993 by an Act of Parliament to investigate and recommend on human rights violations.
questions.option_4: The Chairperson of the NHRC is the Prime Minister of India.

questions.question: According to the principle of collective responsibility in India’s parliamentary system, a successful no-confidence motion against a single Union Minister requires which action?
questions.option_1: The entire Council of Ministers must resign.
questions.option_2: Only the targeted minister must resign.
questions.option_3: The President dismisses only the targeted minister.
questions.option_4: The Lok Sabha is immediately dissolved.

questions.question: Which authority can dissolve the Council of Ministers at the state level?
Which of the following Ministries is responsible for granting Indian citizenship by naturalisation?
Which of the following is NOT a role of the President of India?
Which of the following Articles deals with the rights of citizenship at the commencement of the Indian Constitution?
Which of the following best reflects Judicial Activism?
questions.option_1: Voluntary interference by the judiciary in public interest

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.option_1: It provides for disqualification of members on grounds of defection.
questions.option_2: It defines the distribution of powers between the Union and States.
questions.option_3: It contains provisions regarding Official Languages.
questions.option_4: It deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha.
